skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.description: Requires fresh verification evidence before claiming work is complete, fixed, passing, ready to commit, or ready for review.
skill.summary: Require fresh test, build, lint, or review evidence before completion claims.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Ruby-style backtick execution and weak cryptography, but review found Markdown examples rather than executable code and no cryptographic algorithm. The skill intentionally directs agents to run verification commands, which is legitimate for developer workflows but creates an external command execution risk in untrusted repositories.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed Verification Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run project commands such as tests, builds, linters, type checks, and GitHub PR checks before claiming completion. This is legitimate verification behavior, but it can execute project-defined scripts and should be used only in trusted workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs agents to run full commands and lists concrete command examples. The purpose is legitimate verification, so this is elevated operational risk rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Matches Are Markdown
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in fenced code blocks, inline command examples, and Markdown evidence templates. No executable Ruby code or shell interpolation was found in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows Markdown prose and examples, not runnable source code. The package contains only SKILL.md, so there is no separate executable implementation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The high static finding at SKILL.md line 3 was not supported by the reviewed content. The line is YAML front matter text describing verification discipline and contains no cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review of line 3 found only a natural-language description. No hash, cipher, certificate, or cryptographic API usage appears in the skill file.

content.value_statement: AI agents can overstate completion without current evidence. This skill requires fresh verification commands and evidence before success claims, commits, or pull requests.
actual_capabilities: Requires fresh command execution before completion claims.
Maps common claims to test, build, lint, and type-check commands.
Defines success criteria using exit codes, failure counts, and output summaries.
Provides a structured evidence format for completion reports.
Adds CC-DevFlow exit gate and task completion checks.
limitations: Does not choose safe commands automatically for every repository.
Depends on the agent having permission to run local tooling.
Cannot prove correctness beyond the executed verification scope.
May increase task time when tests or builds are slow.

use_cases.title: Pre-commit Verification
use_cases.description: Require tests, builds, or linters to run before an agent says a change is ready to commit.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Readiness
use_cases.description: Ask an agent to include command output and exit codes before opening or updating a pull request.

use_cases.title: Flow Gate Enforcement
use_cases.description: Apply consistent verification checkpoints across product, development, QA, and release flow stages.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Completion Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a small coding change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before you say this task is complete, identify the right verification command, run it fresh, and report the exit code and result.

prompt_templates.title: Bug Fix Evidence
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for confirming a defect fix.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show the verification evidence for this bug fix. Include the command, exit code, and whether the original failure is now passing.

prompt_templates.title: Release Gate Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for release or PR readiness.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check release readiness using the project tests, build, lint, and PR checks. Do not claim readiness until each result is confirmed.

prompt_templates.title: Verification Gap Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for reviewing agent claims.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the latest completion message for unsupported claims. Identify missing verification, stale evidence, and commands that must be rerun.

output_examples.input: The agent finished a feature and wants to say all tests pass.
output_examples.output: The response names the test command, reports exit code zero, summarizes pass and fail counts, and then states the claim.

output_examples.input: A build command fails during verification.
output_examples.output: The response refuses to call the task complete, summarizes the failure, and lists the next fix or investigation step.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks whether a pull request is ready.
output_examples.output: The response checks current CI or local verification, records the evidence, and separates verified facts from remaining risks.

best_practices: Run the full relevant command immediately before making a completion claim.
Report exit codes, failure counts, and short output summaries in the final response.
Choose commands that match the claim, such as tests for behavior and builds for packaging.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on cached output or memory from an earlier run.
Do not say tests pass when only a subset was executed.
Do not mark work complete when verification failed or was skipped.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It reduces unsupported completion claims by requiring current command output before an agent says work is done.

faq.question: Does it run commands by itself?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to identify and run appropriate verification commands in the current project context.

faq.question: Is it safe for any repository?
faq.answer: Use caution in untrusted repositories because project test or build scripts can execute arbitrary local commands.

faq.question: What evidence should the agent provide?
faq.answer: The agent should provide the command, exit code, output summary, and a clear conclusion tied to that evidence.

faq.question: Can it verify non-code work?
faq.answer: Yes, if the project has relevant checks, documentation builds, validators, or review criteria that can be run or inspected.

faq.question: Does it replace continuous integration?
faq.answer: No. It encourages local or immediate verification before relying on CI as a final independent check.
